name: CASEY CHAN BLDG E1
address: 62968 OB RILEY ROAD BLDG E1, BEND, OR 97701

fr.elements: Main Building; Sidewalk Network; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response at this property could benefit from ensuring that pathways within the sidewalk network are kept clear to facilitate unobstructed responder access and movement. Trimming and managing dense vegetation surrounding key areas could improve visibility and allow for more efficient navigation during emergency operations. Establishing clear and well-marked access points to the rear of the property, or creating alternative routes, may enhance movement, enabling responders to reach all necessary areas with greater ease. Providing clear signage or indicators of access routes could further support effective staging and coordination of emergency activities. Regular monitoring and maintenance of these elements could collectively contribute to better responder safety and operational efficiency.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Brush Adjacent to Structure; Open Terrain; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Limited Defensible Space; Dense Tree Canopy
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the distance between structures and adjacent vegetation, particularly in areas where dense trees and brush are present, could support both firefighter access and suppression efforts. Managing and thinning the dense tree canopy surrounding structures may reduce fuel continuity and limit potential fire spread to the structure. In areas where vegetation directly borders access routes, continued maintenance will preserve clear pathways for emergency vehicles. Additionally, where clusters of trees exist, strategic thinning could further reduce fuel concentrations and improve wildfire resilience. Maintaining the open terrain and creating strategic breaks within the vegetated perimeter could further support fire suppression operations and protect structures. By integrating these approaches, property preparedness and long-term resilience against wildfires could be significantly improved.
